questions.question: Define Machine Learning and explain its importance in modern technology.
keyPoints: Machine Learning enables computers to learn from data without explicit programming
Powers applications like recommendation systems, medical diagnosis, image recognition
Transformative technology driving AI advancement in 21st century

questions.question: Distinguish between Supervised and Unsupervised Learning with examples.
keyPoints: Supervised: learns from labeled data with input-output pairs
Unsupervised: learns from unlabeled data to find hidden patterns
Examples: Classification, Regression for supervised; Clustering, Dimensionality reduction for unsupervised

questions.question: What is the difference between Classification and Regression? Give one example of each.
keyPoints: Classification predicts discrete categories/classes
Regression predicts continuous numerical values
Example classification: Email spam detection
Example regression: House price prediction

questions.question: Explain the concept of Overfitting and Underfitting in machine learning.
keyPoints: Overfitting: model learns training data too well including noise
Underfitting: model too simple to capture data patterns
Both lead to poor generalization on unseen data
Solution: proper validation, regularization, cross-validation

questions.question: What is the purpose of splitting data into training and testing sets?
keyPoints: Training set: used to fit/train the ML model (70-80% of data)
Testing set: used to evaluate model performance on unseen data (20-30%)
Ensures model generalizes well to new data
Prevents overestimation of model performance

questions.question: Explain K-Means clustering algorithm and its applications.
keyPoints: Unsupervised learning algorithm that partitions data into K clusters
Algorithm: Initialize centroids, assign points to nearest centroid, update centroids, repeat until convergence
Applications: Customer segmentation, image compression, document clustering

questions.question: What is Principal Component Analysis (PCA)? State its significance.
keyPoints: Dimensionality reduction technique transforming correlated features
Creates principal components that capture maximum variance
Reduces computational complexity and helps visualize high-dimensional data
Used in face recognition, data compression, exploratory analysis

questions.question: Explain the supervised learning paradigm in detail. Discuss classification and regression with suitable examples and algorithms.
keyPoints: Supervised learning uses labeled training data to learn mapping function
Classification: predicts discrete categories - examples spam detection, disease diagnosis
Algorithms: Decision Trees, Random Forest, SVM, Logistic Regression
Regression: predicts continuous values - examples price prediction, temperature forecasting
Algorithms: Linear Regression, Polynomial Regression
Evaluation metrics: Accuracy, Precision, Recall for classification; MSE, R-squared for regression

questions.question: Discuss the unsupervised learning paradigm. Explain clustering and dimensionality reduction with examples and algorithms.
keyPoints: Unsupervised learning finds patterns in unlabeled data without predefined outputs
Clustering groups similar data points - algorithms: K-Means, Hierarchical, DBSCAN
Applications: customer segmentation, image segmentation, anomaly detection
Dimensionality reduction reduces features - algorithms: PCA, t-SNE, LDA
Helps in visualization, noise reduction, and computational efficiency

questions.question: Explain the concepts of bias and variance. How do they relate to overfitting and underfitting? Discuss techniques to address these issues.
keyPoints: Bias: error from overly simplistic assumptions (underfitting)
Variance: error from too much complexity/ sensitivity to training data (overfitting)
High bias + high variance: worst case - neither learns nor generalizes
Techniques: cross-validation, regularization (L1/L2), pruning, early stopping
Model selection: proper validation set, hyperparameter tuning
